title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Healthcare
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for healthcare practices. Every quote gets chased on time, in your voice, without an extra hire.
excerpt: Healthcare proposals and quotes get sent, then sit. The AIOS chases each one on a steady schedule, in your voice, so more turn into signed work.

sections.intro:
You run a healthcare practice or service company. You send a quote for a new patient program, a corporate wellness contract, an equipment lease, or a referral arrangement, and then it goes quiet. The decision maker is busy, your front desk is busy, and the quote slips. A week later you remember it, send a polite check-in, and hope.

This is the part of the business that quietly leaks revenue. The work to write the proposal is already done. The only thing missing is a steady, respectful follow-up that does not depend on anyone remembering. The AIOS handles that. It watches every proposal and quote you send, follows up on a schedule, in your voice, and tells you the moment something needs a human reply.
sections.fourCFit:
Proposal and Quote Follow-Up sits in the Capabilities layer of the AIOS. It is a done-for-you workflow that runs on top of the rest of the system.

  • Context: your services, pricing structure, payer notes, and the tone you use with prospects and referral partners.
  • Connections: your inbox, CRM or practice management system, document or e-sign tool, and calendar.
  • Capabilities: this workflow, plus the 60-Second Lead Engine upstream so new leads never go cold.
  • Cadence: a steady follow-up rhythm that runs whether you are in clinic, in surgery, or off for the weekend.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
You get one specific artifact: a follow-up engine attached to every quote and proposal you send.

  • Every sent proposal is logged automatically with value, recipient, and date.
  • First follow-up goes out within 2 business days, in your voice, referencing the specific service quoted.
  • Second touch at day 5, third at day 10, final at day 17, each one written for healthcare buyers, not generic sales copy.
  • HIPAA-aware phrasing: no patient identifiers in outbound messages, no clinical details in subject lines.
  • When the prospect replies, the sequence stops and the message is routed to you or your office manager with a short summary and a suggested next step.
  • A weekly digest lands in your inbox: quotes out, quotes accepted, quotes stalled, dollars in play.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We look at how proposals leave your practice today, where they live, and where they stall. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We connect your inbox, CRM, and document tool, write the follow-up sequences in your voice, set the HIPAA-aware guardrails, and test against your last 30 to 60 quotes before going live. See plans.
  3. Operate (ongoing). We monitor the workflow, tune the messaging based on what actually books, and add one new automation per week to the rest of your AIOS.
sections.expectedResults:
• Proposal response rate typically moves from 20 to 35 percent up to 45 to 60 percent within 60 days, because nothing falls through anymore.
  • Time to close on accepted quotes drops by 25 to 40 percent, since the next nudge always lands on time.
  • Office manager or owner time spent chasing quotes drops to near zero, usually 4 to 8 hours per week returned.
  • You see every stalled deal in one weekly view, so revenue forecasting gets honest.
  • Standing guarantee: we recover 40 or more hours a month across your AIOS or we keep working free until we do.
sections.faq:
Is this compliant with HIPAA and patient privacy rules?

Yes. Follow-up messages are written to never include protected health information. We work with your compliance lead during install to confirm what can and cannot appear in outbound communication, and we keep a written record of the rules the system follows.

Will the follow-ups sound like a robot wrote them?

No. We draft the sequences in your voice using real examples from your past sent folder. You approve every template before it goes live. If a reply needs nuance, it stops the sequence and routes to a human.

What about quotes that are not for patients, like vendor proposals or B2B contracts?

The same workflow handles both. We segment by deal type during install, so a corporate wellness proposal gets a different tone and cadence than a referral partnership or an equipment quote.
